headline: Gemini App Rolls Out Selectable Thinking Levels
summary: Google began rolling out a 'Thinking level' option inside the Gemini app, letting users choose between Fast, Standard, and Extended reasoning modes when using Gemini 3.1 Pro. Extended mode enables deeper chain-of-thought processing at higher compute cost, while Fast prioritises speed and low latency.
foryou: A per-request thinking budget knob is exactly what agentic pipelines need — use Fast for cheap tool-dispatch steps and Extended only for the planning/reasoning node, cutting your per-run cost significantly.

headline: OpenAI's Codex to Gain Computer Use Across Desktop Devices
summary: OpenAI confirmed that Codex will soon be able to control other desktop devices via its Computer Use capability, extending it beyond browser environments. The move positions Codex as a cross-device agentic coding assistant able to interact with local IDEs, terminals, and other applications.
foryou: Codex with cross-device Computer Use means your code-review and test-run loops can be fully autonomous — it can open your local test runner, read the output, and push a fix without any human handoff.

headline: ChatGPT Launches Personal Finance Features
summary: OpenAI rolled out a personal finance capability in ChatGPT, allowing users to connect financial accounts and get AI-driven analysis of spending, savings, and investments. The feature brings ChatGPT into direct competition with Mint-style personal finance apps and robo-advisors.
foryou: As ChatGPT ingests real financial data, it becomes a serious competitor in the personal finance agent space — and a preview of the kind of tool-connected, account-aware assistants that will dominate consumer AI.

headline: DeepSeek-V4-Flash Makes LLM Activation Steering Relevant Again
summary: Engineer Sean Goedecke argues that DeepSeek-V4-Flash's architecture makes steering vectors — a technique for directly modifying model behaviour by adding activations during inference — practically useful again after years of limited applicability. The post includes working examples of steering personality and refusal behaviour.
foryou: If you want fine-grained runtime control over model behaviour without re-training, steering vectors on DeepSeek-V4-Flash are now worth adding to your evaluation toolkit.

headline: OpenAI Quietly Acquired Voice-Cloning Startup Weights.gg and Folded the Team
summary: OpenAI acquired Weights.gg, a voice-cloning startup, without a public announcement and absorbed its team into internal projects. The acquisition signals OpenAI's continued push into voice and audio AI capabilities beyond the text-to-speech features already in ChatGPT.

headline: Runway Pivots From Filmmakers to Competing With Google on AI
summary: TechCrunch profiles Runway's strategic shift: the company that pioneered AI video generation for creative professionals is now targeting enterprise and developer markets, positioning itself as a direct competitor to Google's Veo and other foundation video model providers.

headline: The 62.5-Minute Rule for Claude's Prompt Cache
summary: A developer's deep-dive reveals that Claude's server-side prompt cache expires after exactly 62.5 minutes of inactivity — not the round 60 or 90 minutes most assume. Structuring your system-prompt refresh cadence to stay inside this window can cut repeated token costs by up to 90% on long-running agentic sessions.
foryou: If your scheduled tasks or always-on agents call Claude more than once per hour, keep the cache alive by pinging within 62 minutes — one cheap 'ping' call saves you re-billing a 10k-token system prompt every time.

headline: How Claude Code Works in Large Codebases: Best Practices from Anthropic
summary: Anthropic published an official guide on using Claude Code effectively in large repositories, covering memory file organisation, parallel sub-agent delegation, and how to scope context to avoid hitting limits. The guide is based on how Anthropic's own teams use it in production.
foryou: The memory file and CLAUDE.md patterns described here are the fastest path to making Claude Code useful in a real project — skip the trial and error and read the org's own playbook.
